title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 11.
content:
There was an Old Man with a flute,
    A sarpint ran into his boot;
    But he played day and night,
    Till the sarpint took flight,
    And avoided that man with a flute.
meaning: About: Edward Lear was an English artist, illustrator and writer known for his literary nonsense, in poetry and prose, and especially his limericks, a form which he popularised.
